skill.name: Azure Event Hubs SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure Event Hubs SDK for Python streaming. Use for high-throughput event ingestion, producers, consumers, and checkpointing.
skill.summary: Reference skill for Azure Event Hubs Python SDK - send and receive events with producers, consumers, and checkpoint stores
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill containing reference material for Azure Event Hubs Python SDK. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) as this is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. No security issues detected. The skill provides legitimate technical documentation for Azure Event Hubs, a standard cloud messaging service.

content.user_title: Integrate Azure Event Hubs with Python
content.value_statement: Build high-throughput event streaming pipelines with Azure Event Hubs Python SDK. This skill provides code patterns for producers, consumers, and checkpoint stores.
actual_capabilities: Create EventHubProducerClient to send events to Event Hub
Create EventHubConsumerClient to receive events from Event Hub
Use BlobCheckpointStore for tracking consumer progress
Implement async clients for high-throughput scenarios
Send events to specific partitions using partition keys
Get Event Hub properties and partition information
limitations: Requires valid Azure Event Hub namespace and credentials
Does not create or manage Event Hub resources
Does not handle application-level error recovery
Requires blob storage account for checkpoint store

use_cases.title: Build event ingestion pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a producer that sends high-volume events from IoT devices or applications to Azure Event Hubs
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building real-time data pipelines

use_cases.title: Process streaming events
use_cases.description: Build a consumer application that receives and processes events with checkpointing for reliable delivery

use_cases.title: Implement async event processing
use_cases.description: Use async clients to handle high-throughput event streaming with better resource utilization

prompt_templates.title: Send events to Event Hub
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an EventHubProducerClient and send a batch of events using azure-eventhub Python SDK

prompt_templates.title: Receive events with checkpointing
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up an EventHubConsumerClient with BlobCheckpointStore to receive events and track progress?

prompt_templates.title: Async event streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the async version of EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient for high-throughput scenarios

prompt_templates.title: Partition and routing
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I send events to specific partitions using partition_id or partition_key with azure-eventhub?

output_examples.output: Code that creates an EventHubProducerClient, uses create_batch() to batch events, handles batch size limits with try/except, and sends in batches
Setup with EventHubConsumerClient, BlobCheckpointStore, on_event callback that updates checkpoint after processing each event
Call get_eventhub_properties() to retrieve partition IDs, then get_partition_properties() for each partition's details

best_practices: Use batches when sending multiple events to reduce network calls
Use checkpoint stores in production for reliable event processing
Use async clients for high-throughput scenarios
anti_patterns: Sending events one at a time without batching
Not updating checkpoints causing duplicate processing on restart
Not using context managers for client connections

faq.question: What is Azure Event Hubs?
faq.answer: Azure Event Hubs is a big data streaming platform and event ingestion service from Microsoft Azure. It can stream millions of events per second.

faq.question: What is the difference between EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient?
faq.answer: ProducerClient sends events to Event Hub. ConsumerClient receives events from Event Hub. They are used on opposite sides of the streaming pipeline.

faq.question: What is checkpointing?
faq.answer: Checkpointing tracks the last processed event in a partition. It uses BlobCheckpointStore to persist the position so consumers can resume from where they left off.

faq.question: What is a partition key?
faq.answer: A partition key is a string that ensures all events with the same key go to the same partition. This maintains ordering for related events.

faq.question: Do I need Azure Blob Storage for Event Hubs?
faq.answer: Blob storage is required only when using checkpoint stores. For simple consumers without checkpointing, you do not need blob storage.

faq.question: What authentication does Azure Event Hubs support?
faq.answer: The SDK uses DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities, service principals, and other Azure authentication methods.
